summary: add `temper re-add` — reconcile on-disk harness edits back into the surface (the third drift direction)

per.section: Drift / apply — three states, never two (the hard core)

new.description: Acceptance: a drifted source is pulled into the surface (body + meta.toml rewritten, lock fingerprints refreshed); an added on-disk source becomes a new surface artifact + lock row; an in-sync harness is a no-op; covers skill and rule kinds.

edit.description: Add the `re_add` write direction beside `diff`/`apply`: reuse `diff`'s four-state classification to target the drifted + added built-in artifacts, re-parse each source through the skill/rule loaders, project it into its surface tree, and refresh the lock row's import_hash/body_hash/last_applied to the current source bytes. Report types (ReAddReport/ReAddEntry/outcome) + `render_readd`, mirroring the apply report.

Expose the per-kind surface writers (import_skill/import_rule) as pub(crate) so re-add reuses the single round-trip write path (meta.toml via to_meta_document + byte-faithful body) rather than re-implementing it. Visibility only — no signature or behavior change, existing import tests untouched.

Add `Command::ReAdd { harness_path, into }` + dispatch: load the surface, run `drift::re_add` against the live harness, print the report, exit zero (a reconcile, not a gate). Mirrors the `Diff` variant's shape.

tests.asserts: A drifted skill/rule source is reconciled into the surface (SKILL.md/RULE.md body + meta.toml updated, lock import_hash/last_applied bumped to the new bytes); an added on-disk source gains a surface dir + lock row; an unchanged harness leaves every surface byte identical.

acceptance: `temper re-add <harness>` updates the surface from drifted/added on-disk sources and refreshes the lock; the existing diff/apply tests stay green.
notes: Completes the drift trilogy (diff/apply/re-add) law 6 rests on. Forks surface-authority + workspace-scope are RESOLVED. Built-in kinds only (skill/rule), matching diff/apply's scope; generic custom-kind re-add is follow-on. Shares main.rs with SESSION-START-GATE, which is serialized behind it.

summary: add the advisory session-start gate — a one-shot check over a harness path emitting the claude-session-start reporter payload

per.section: Decision: the session-start gate is advisory, not blocking

new.description: The `claude-session-start` reporter: render the merged diagnostic set to the SessionStart JSON payload — a failing contract emits `additionalContext` carrying the verdict plus a notify-and-approve instruction; a clean harness emits a quiet payload. Capped to Claude Code's 10k additionalContext limit.

Acceptance over the reporter + the one-shot verb: a failing contract yields a payload with the verdict + notify-and-approve instruction; a clean harness yields a quiet payload; output is valid JSON under the 10k cap; the run exits 0 (advisory, never blocks).

edit.description: Add `pub mod reporter;` (the foundation line for the new module — this is the only entry that touches lib.rs).

Add a session-start check verb that takes a harness path, imports it internally (one-shot, not the two-step author check), runs the same by-kind gate `check` runs, and emits the reporter payload on stdout; advisory — exit 0 regardless. The hook IS the binary (exec-form), so no shell wrapper.

tests.asserts: The emitted SessionStart payload is valid JSON ≤10k, carries the verdict + notify-and-approve instruction on a failing harness and stays quiet on a clean one, and the process exits 0 (advisory).

acceptance: `temper` produces the SessionStart hook payload for a harness path via the claude-session-start reporter; the gate is advisory (never blocks).
notes: 50-distribution.md: the hook is the temper binary itself, one-shot over a harness path (import-internally). Fail-loud (a hook that cannot run temper) is a separate concern. SARIF + GitHub-annotation reporters are sibling follow-ons on the same reporter family. Serialized behind RE-ADD only because both touch main.rs.
